scope.goal: Complete the constructor/init side of the 0x38-byte locale/default object lifecycle discovered from the shared locale-helper milestone.
scope.relationship_to_previous_milestone: The prior shared-helper pass identified 0x1407B6120 as the 0x38-byte allocator, 0x1407B5D8C as its constructor target, 0x1407B6150 as the post-allocation singleton publisher, and 0x1407B5E14 as the string/field copier used by locale-name helper 0x1407B6098.

constructor_0x1407b5d8c.role: Constructor-like initializer for the 0x38-byte locale/default object allocated by 0x1407B6120.
constructor_0x1407b5d8c.confidence: high for object size, field layout, descriptor install, and only direct caller; medium for exact public type name.
observed_behavior: Copies default marker 0x140B9CD84 into [object+0x28] by measuring a null-terminated byte string, allocating through 0x1407CD068, and copying through 0x1407B9C90.

constant_readback.0x140B9CD84: ASCII '*' at the start of the rdata region, followed by adjacent unrelated strings; retained as wildcard/default marker pending wider table mapping.

field_string_copier_0x1407b5e14.role: Reusable owned-byte-string field setter used by the locale-name helper family.
field_string_copier_0x1407b5e14.confidence: high for behavior and caller; medium for exact allocator/copy helper labels.
direct_callers_observed: 0x1407B60C6 from helper 0x1407B6098, plus a tail jump from 0x1407B6098 into 0x1407B5E14 for [object+0x58]
observed_behavior: Takes RCX as the destination pointer field address and RDX as the source C-string pointer.
If the old destination pointer equals the source pointer, returns without changing it.
If an old destination pointer exists, frees it through 0x1407C4634 and clears the field.
If the new source is null, leaves the field null.
Otherwise measures the source including the null terminator, allocates through 0x1407CD068, stores the new pointer, and copies bytes through 0x1407B9C90.

singleton_publisher_0x1407b6150.role: Publishes a newly initialized default object into global slot 0x140C55928 and registers one-time cleanup.
singleton_publisher_0x1407b6150.confidence: high for publication and one-time callback registration; medium for exact cleanup framework name.
observed_behavior: On first execution, sets the guard to 1, loads callback 0x1407B61C0, and registers it through 0x1407B7B64.
Stores the incoming object pointer into global default slot 0x140C55928.

related_globals.0x140C55928: Default object/global slot loaded by 0x1407B5F20 and 0x1407B5F28, stored by 0x1407B6150, and passed by address to cleanup callback 0x1407B61C0.
related_globals.0x140C5592F: One-time registration guard byte written by 0x1407B6150.
related_globals.0x140C55927: Byte cleared by cleanup callback 0x1407B61C0 after invoking 0x1407B6184.

cleanup_callback_0x1407b61c0_and_target_.role: Registered cleanup callback for the default object slot.
cleanup_callback_0x1407b61c0_and_target_.confidence: high for callback registration and global slot cleanup; medium for exact lifecycle framework.

direct_callers_observed.0x1407B61C0: No direct CALL references; address is passed to 0x1407B7B64 by 0x1407B6150.

observed_behavior: 0x1407B61C0 enters guard helper 0x1407B6330, passes address 0x140C55928 into 0x1407B6184, clears byte 0x140C55927, then exits through 0x1407B63A8.
0x1407B6184 takes a pointer to a pointer, loads the pointed object, and returns if it is null.
When the object is present, 0x1407B6184 calls vtable slot [object_vtable+0x10] through dispatch pointer 0x140803060.
If that call returns a non-null object, 0x1407B6184 dispatches into the returned object's vtable with EDX=1, matching a delete/finalize style call.

0x140806700.role: Descriptor/vtable row installed by constructor 0x1407B5D8C and by the destructor-like state transition at 0x1407B5E8C.

0x1408066E0.role: Sibling/base descriptor row installed after cleanup in the destructor-like routine.

0x140806D18.role: Rdata row pointing at dispatch table pointer 0x140803060; qword readback confirms 0x140803060 begins with executable targets 0x1407F4430 and 0x1407F4450.

adjacent_wrappers_and_builders.0x1407B5BF8: Tiny virtual-dispatch wrapper that calls vtable slot [object_vtable+0x18] through dispatch pointer 0x140803060 and returns zero; 20 direct callers observed.
adjacent_wrappers_and_builders.0x1407B5C38: Tiny virtual-dispatch wrapper that calls vtable slot [object_vtable+0x10] through dispatch pointer 0x140803060 and returns zero; 24 direct callers observed.
adjacent_wrappers_and_builders.0x1407B5C50: Stack-cookie protected builder/bridge with 5 direct callers; it uses 0x14002F100, 0x1407B5174, 0x1407B4F38, 0x1407B4E3C, 0x1407B5208, indirect vtable dispatches, and cookie check 0x1407B8BE0.
adjacent_wrappers_and_builders.0x1407B5D38: Smaller builder/bridge with 13 direct callers; it shares 0x14002F100, 0x1407B5174, an indirect dispatch through 0x140803060, and 0x1407B5208.

tooling_updates.note: No Reverser decoder changes were required for this milestone; all lifecycle windows decoded cleanly with the current native instruction support.

next_targets: Trace 0x1407B7B64 and guard-slot helpers 0x1407B7BA0, 0x1407B7BA8, 0x1407B7BB8, and 0x1407B7BC0 to name the registration/guard framework around default object cleanup.
Map helper cluster 0x1407B4E3C, 0x1407B4F38, 0x1407B5174, and 0x1407B5208 to identify the adjacent builder/bridge wrappers 0x1407B5C50 and 0x1407B5D38.
Follow dispatch pointer 0x140803060 and targets 0x1407F4430/0x1407F4450 enough to classify the indirect-call mechanism used across this locale/facet area.
